channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.ign.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.ign.com

item.title: LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight Reveals Gotham by Gaslight Batsuit
item.content:encoded:
LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight is pulling from everything in the world of Batman: the movies, the 1960's TV show, and of course the comics. Today, WB Games has revealed the LEGO Batman version of the Batsuit from Gotham by Gaslight, the beloved steampunk-themed Elsewords comic from 1989.

The hugely popular steampunk-era Batman comic pit Batman circa-1889 against Jack the Ripper. The LEGO version of this era-appropriate Batsuit is, as you can see in the trailer below, a bit more playful than the dark tone of that comic, in keeping with the LEGO theme.

Check out IGN's hands-on impressions of Legacy of the Dark Knight, plus 31 minutes of gameplay. LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will be released on all platforms on May 22.

Ryan McCaffrey is IGN's executive editor of previews and host of both IGN's weekly Xbox show, Podcast Unlocked, as well as our semi-retired interview show, IGN Unfiltered. He's a North Jersey guy, so it's "Taylor ham," not "pork roll." Debate it with him on Twitter at @DMC_Ryan.



https://www.ign.com/articles/lego-batman-legacy-of-the-dark-knight-reveals-gotham-by-gaslight-batsuit
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hasbro Is Making Marvel Rivals Action Figures, and They're Just as Huge As You'd Expect
item.content:encoded:
Hasbro's Marvel Legends line may focus mostly on delivering figures based on Marvel's comic book, film, and TV projects, but sometimes they branch out into games as well. And given the massive popularity of the hero shooter Marvel Rivals, it should come as no surprise that the company is revealing a wave of Marvel Legends figures based on that game.

Check out the slideshow gallery below for a closer look at Hasbro's Marvel Legends Series: Marvel Rivals figures:

Marvel Legends Series: Marvel Rivals Figures - Image Gallery

As you can see, these new figures are a bit of a stylistic departure from the usual Marvel Legends format, mimicking the extremely muscular and stylized character designs the game is known for. And, of course, Jeff the Land Shark is one of the characters who made the cut.

This wave of figures includes:

  • Moon Knight
  • Punisher
  • Spider-Man & Luna Snow 2-Pack
  • Venom & Jeff the Land Shark 2-Pack

All of these Marvel Rivals figures will be released in Fall 2026. Preorders will be staggered over the next several weeks, with preorder links going live on Hasbro Pulse and other websites between April 22 and May 13, depending on the figure.

Obviously, this lineup barely scratches the surface of the massive character roster in the game. Which hero or villain do you most want to see as a figure? Let us know in the comments below.

Previously, Hasbro released a series of Marvel Gamerverse figures inspired by the Marvel vs. Capcom series.

In other Marvel Rivals news, the developer confirmed that a Nintendo Switch 2 version is in the works. Don't expect any original characters to be added to the game, though, as there are simply too many Marvel characters to cover first.

Jesse is a mild-mannered staff writer for IGN. Allow him to lend a machete to your intellectual thicket by following @jschedeen on BlueSky.



https://www.ign.com/articles/hasbro-is-making-marvel-rivals-action-figures-and-theyre-just-as-huge-as-youd-expect
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Exclusive: Wilds Unknown's Sid Phillips, Alien, and The Claw Are Some of the First-Ever Toy Story and Pixar Disney Lorcana Cards
item.content:encoded:
Also get a look at Babyhead, Hand-In-The-Box, and Bouncing Ducky!

Pixar and Toy Story are joining Disney Lorcana for the first time with its Wilds Unknown expansion, and we are excited to exclusively reveal six cards from the set, including Sid Phillips - Toy Surgeon, Alien - True Believer, The Claw, Babyhead - Leader of Sid's Toys, Hand-In-The-Box - Sid's Toy, and Bouncing Ducky - Sid's Toy.

Disney Lorcana's Wilds Unknown expansion set will debut at local game stores on May 8 and everywhere else on May 15, and it marks a big moment for the trading card game. Not only are Toy Story cards on their way, but we'll also get cards from The Incredibles as well. Furthermore, the Toy Story cards come with their own new classification, aptly named Toy.

"Cards with the Toy classification in Wilds Unknown get better the more toys you have out, and many of the toy cards have tricks to help you play themselves or even other cards for cheaper," Levi Dodd, Senior Game Designer at Ravensburger for Disney Lorcana Trading Card Game, told us. "But the neat thing is that toys don't just care about other toys, some of them have abilities that care about playing other cards with a small ink cost. Any way you play a card for 2 ink or less will work, so try out toys together with songs, cost reduction, or just a lot of low-cost glimmers!"

And obviously, the toys are the stars. Buzz Lightyear - On the Way has already been revealed and we can imagine we'll be getting Woody (as he appears in the art) and our other favorites, but we're honored to be able to reveal some of the cards you'll soon be able to add to your collection.

While Alien - True Believer and The Claw are just the perfect match and both look great, Sid Phillips - Toy Surgeon is an important card for so many reasons.

"The design team made the glimmer of Sid Philips – Toy Surgeon and his toys all Ruby glimmers since Sid has a bit of a chaotic side," Dodd explained. "He fit right in! We knew early on his abilities would be about banishing characters, even his own, but it wasn’t until later that we thought to add in his second ability, which gives you lore when a Toy is banished. We thought it was the perfect way to represent how the glimmer loves to experiment with his toys.

"As for glimmers of his toys, they are all about recycling their friends back into your deck, just like they repair each other in the movie."

There is so much more to look forward to in Wilds Unknown and beyond, and we're just about to get a sneak peek at what's next with today's special Wilds Unknown Special Lorecast at 10am PT / 1pm ET! We'll be covering all the big news from the show right here on IGN, so stay tuned!

For more, check out our impressions of Winterspell's Illuminier's Trove, the top chase cards for Winterspell and Fabled and what else to expect from Lorcana in 2026.

Adam Bankhurst is a writer for IGN. You can follow him on X/Twitter @AdamBankhurst, Instagram, and TikTok, and listen to his show, Talking Disney Magic.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/exclusive-wilds-unknowns-sid-phillips-alien-and-the-claw-are-some-of-the-first-ever-toy-story-and-pixar-disney-lorcana-cards
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 99 Nights in the Forest Film Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
20th Century Studios has snagged the rights to make a movie based on hit Roblox game 99 Nights in the Forest.

99 Nights in the Forest is the seventh most-played game of all time on Roblox despite being only about a year old. As you could probably guess from the title, it has a bit of a Five Nights at Freddy’s feel to it, forcing players to spend 99 nights in a haunted forest. During that time, players must defeat creatures that live in the woods and set out to save four lost children.

The game currently has 26 billion lifetime visits and a 14.2 million all-time peak player count, according to The Hollywood Reporter. Hearing those numbers, it makes a lot of sense as to why 20th Century Studios decided to make it its first major foray into video game adaptations since Disney acquired the studio in 2019.

No creatives or producers have been attached to the project yet beyond the game’s developers — Alex Kieft, Cameron Angland, and Matthew Hufton — who will serve as executive producers.

“Growing up, Disney and 20th films were a huge part of our childhood, so collaborating with such legendary and enduring studios feels surreal,” the game’s creators said in a statement. “We’re excited to explore the world of 99 Nights in the Forest on a broader stage and to create some scares in an entirely new medium.”

Lex Briscuso is a film and television critic and a freelance entertainment writer for IGN. You can follow her on Twitter at @nikonamerica.



https://www.ign.com/articles/99-nights-in-the-forest-film-confirmed
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
